questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q: Which of the following plots represent an ideal binary solution of two volatile liquids $A$ and $B$ ?
options: Plot of $P_{\text {total }}$ vs $X_B$ is linear
Plot of $P_{\text {total }}$ vs $Y_A$ is linear
Plot of $1 / P_{\text {total }} v s Y_A / X_A$ is linear
Plot of $1 / P_{\text {total }} v s Y_B / X_B$ is non linear
questions.solution:
Total vapour pressure $\left(P_T\right)$ is given by
$P_T=P_A^{\circ}+\left(P_B^{\circ}-P_A^{\circ}\right) X_B$

So, plot of $P_T$ vs $X_B$ is linear with a slope of $\left(P_B^{\circ}-\right.$ $\left.P_A^o\right)$
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.q: Consider the following statements and select the correct ones.
options: For bromoethane $(A)$ and chloroethane $(B)$ solution intermolecular interactions of $A-A$ and $B-B$ type are almost equal to $A-B$ type
For ethanol and acetone mixture $\Delta \mathrm{H}_{\text {Solution }}=+\mathrm{ve}$
For HCl and NaOH mixture $\Delta \mathrm{V}_{\text {Solution }}=-\mathrm{ve}$
Ethanol and acetone mixture gives minimum boiling point azeotrope

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q:
Consider the following values of $\mathrm{K}_{\mathrm{H}}$ for given gases at $293 \mathrm{~K} .\left(\mathrm{K}_{\mathrm{H}}=\right.$ Henry's constant, $\mathrm{K}_{\mathrm{H}}$ is given in bar)
$\mathrm{He}=144.97, \quad \mathrm{H}_2=69.16, \quad \mathrm{~N}_2=76.48, \quad \mathrm{O}_2=34.86$

Select the correct option(s).
options: Correct order of solubility is $\mathrm{He}\gt\mathrm{N}_2\gt\mathrm{H}_2\gt\mathrm{O}_2$ in water if each gas has partial pressure 1 atm
Correct order of solubility is $\mathrm{O}_2\gt\mathrm{H}_2\gt\mathrm{N}_2\gt\mathrm{He}$ in water if each gas has partial pressure 1 atm
Correct order of mole fraction is $\mathrm{N}_2\gt\mathrm{O}_2$ when air is dissolved in water, if air consist $80 \% \mathrm{~N}_2$ and $20 \%$ $\mathrm{O}_2$ and total pressure of air is 1 atm
If partial pressure of $\mathrm{O}_2$ and $\mathrm{N}_2$ are 1 atm , then mole fraction of $\mathrm{N}_2$ is 0.46 (approx) times that of $\mathrm{O}_2$
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q: Which of the following statement(s) is/are correct?
options: Colligative properties of 1 L of 1 M HCl solution decrease when equal volume of 1 M NaOH is mixed
Vapour pressure of 3 molal aqueous solution of urea is less than that of 2 molal NaCl aqueous solution at the same temperature
$0.2 \mathrm{M} \mathrm{KCl}(\mathrm{aq})\left(80 \%\right.$ dissociation) is isotonic with $0.2 \mathrm{M} \mathrm{CaCl}_2(40 \%$ dissociation) at the same temperature
$0.1 \mathrm{~m} \mathrm{NaNO}_3$ ( $90 \%$ dissociation) has same boiling point as $0.1 \mathrm{~m} \mathrm{FeCl}_3$ (30\% dissociation)
questions.solution:
Concentration if halved so, all the colligative properties of solution will decrease.
Vapour pressure of 3 molal aq. solution of urea will be higher than that of 2 molal aq. NaCl solution because $\mathrm{i}=1$ for urea and $\mathrm{i}=2$ for NaCl
$\begin{aligned}
& \mathrm{KCl} \rightleftharpoons=\mathrm{K}^{+}+\mathrm{Cl}^{-} \\
& 0.2(1-0.8) \quad 0.16 \quad 0.16 \\
& \text { (ic }=0.36 \text { ) } \\
& \mathrm{CaCl}_2 \rightleftharpoons \mathrm{Ca}^{2+}+2 \mathrm{Cl}^{-} \\
& \text {(ic }=0.36 \text { ) }
\end{aligned}$

So, both these solutions are isotonic
$\begin{gathered}
\mathrm{NaNO}_3 \\
0.1(1-0.9) \\
\\
\mathrm{Na}^{+}+\mathrm{NO}_3^{-} \\
\mathrm{NeCl}_3
\end{gathered} \rightleftharpoons \mathrm{Ne}^{3+}+3 \mathrm{Fl}^{-}(\mathrm{ic}=0.19)$

So both these solution have same boiling point.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q: $\mathrm{CCl}_4$ and acetone form a non-ideal solution at room temperature in a copper container. For this process, the true statement(s) is (are) :
options: $\Delta \mathrm{G}$ is positive
$\Delta \mathrm{S}_{\text {system }}$ is positive
$\Delta \mathrm{S}_{\text {surroundings }} \lt 0$
$\Delta \mathrm{H}\gt0$
questions.solution: $\begin{array}{ll}\Delta \mathrm{G}=-\mathrm{ve}, \Delta \mathrm{S}_{\text {system }} & =+ \text { ve } \quad \text { Always for solution formation. } \\ \Delta \mathrm{S}_{\text {surr. }} \lt 0 & \text { Heat absorbed by solution from surrounding. } \\ \Delta \mathrm{H}\gt0 & \text { For this solution as } \mathrm{CCl}_4 \text { is non-polar but } \\ & \text { acetone is polar }\end{array}$
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
